text:
**Filter construction from joined-channels state** — `src/fabric/subscriptions.rs` is a **pure planner** (holds no transport handle, `:171`). Three aggregate filter builders: `aggregate_h_filter` (kinds 9/30315/30023 by `#h`, `:74`), `aggregate_p_filter` (9/30023 by `#p`, `:85`), `aggregate_gstate_filter` (39000/39001/39002 by `#d`, `:96`). `SubscriptionRegistry` holds `aggregate` + `narrow` coverage sets; `seed(coverage)` returns the 3 aggregate REQs `:187`, `add_channel(h)` returns narrow delta REQs (one `#h`, one gstate) or empty if covered `:204`. [...] **Recomputation on channel-set change** — two paths, both in `daemon/server/subscriptions.rs`:
- Incremental: `ensure_subscription(state, project)` (`:3`) pushes to `subscribed_projects`, calls `registry.add_channel` (delta only), and `apply_plan` opens the narrow REQs (5s-bounded, fail-open). Called from `session_start` `:403` and from demux on a kind:39002 where a local pubkey is a member (`demux.rs:132`).
- Full reseed: `resubscribe(state)` (`:172`) calls `build_entity_coverage` (recompute from store) → `registry.seed` → `apply_plan`, replacing the 3 aggregates in place via stable NIP-01 subscription ids (`te-v2-h-all` etc., `subscriptions.rs:37-39`). [...] 1. **Relay subscriptions** — the strongest fit and already half-built. `fabric/subscriptions.rs` is a pure `state → Vec<PlannedReq>` planner returning open/close deltas — exactly a plan. But: (a) it is applied via **two divergent paths** (incremental `add_channel` vs full `seed`) rather than one reconcile; (b) the **close/compact half is dead code** — `SubscriptionRegistry::compact` (`subscriptions.rs:243`) and daemon `close_subs` (`daemon/server/subscriptions.rs:88`) are both `#[allow(dead_code)]`. Consequence: narrow REQs only ever accumulate; nothing ever unsubscribes a channel a session left. A `state → {desired REQ set} → diff against live → open/close + receipt` engine would directly subsume this and fix the leak. [...] - **Teardown machinery is dead code**: `close_subs` (`subscriptions.rs:87-95`, verified) is `#[allow(dead_code)]`, doc'd *"Best-effort per id"*, zero non-test callers. `SubscriptionRegistry::compact` (`src/fabric/subscriptions.rs:243-244`) is likewise `#[allow(dead_code)]`: *"Not yet wired into the daemon — compaction at a quiet boundary is a future optimization."* There is no `remove_channel`.
- **Self-documented history of this exact class of leak**: `src/transport.rs:293-298` — *"random ids leaked an unbounded number of subscriptions into the relay pool ... growing the process to tens of GB over a day"* and `:369-374` — *"The codebase had no unsubscribe path before ... The registry needs this to compact narrow REQs and retire stale subscriptions ... instead of letting the relay-side subscription set grow monotonically."*
